name: BMAD Method MCP Server
what_it_does: BMAD Method MCP server implementing the BMAD (Breakthrough Method of Agile AI-Driven Development) framework — providing structured prompts, personas, and workflows for AI-driven software development. Enables agents to follow the BMAD methodology for product requirement gathering, story creation, architecture design, and iterative development with specialized AI personas (Analyst, Architect, Developer, QA).
use_cases: Generating structured product requirements following BMAD methodology from planning agents
Creating user stories and acceptance criteria from product management agents
Running BMAD architecture review sessions from design agents
Orchestrating multi-persona AI development workflows from project management agents
Following BMAD's iterative story-driven development process from coding agents
Onboarding teams to BMAD framework with structured AI guidance from training agents
not_for: Teams not using the BMAD methodology (pure Scrum, Kanban, or waterfall teams)
Simple one-off coding tasks (BMAD is for structured software development projects)
Teams wanting minimal process overhead — BMAD is intentionally structured
best_when: A team or individual using the BMAD methodology for AI-driven software development — provides the structured workflow, personas, and prompt templates that make BMAD effective.
avoid_when: You prefer lightweight development workflows or don't follow structured agile methodologies — BMAD adds intentional process structure.

auth.notes: No authentication — local MCP server providing BMAD methodology workflows and prompts. No external service required.

pricing.notes: BMAD method is open source. MCP server is free. Community-driven framework.

known_agent_gotchas: Requires familiarity with BMAD methodology to use effectively — learn BMAD concepts first
BMAD workflow is intentionally opinionated — teams with different processes may find it constraining
AI personas (Analyst, Architect, etc.) require context-rich prompting for best results
Story generation quality depends on quality of initial requirements provided
Community methodology — BMAD evolves; MCP may not track latest BMAD updates immediately
Best used with Claude/Claude Code which the BMAD community primarily targets
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local-only. No credentials. No network calls. Project requirements stay local. Safe for confidential product development work.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local only. No external data transmission. Requirements and stories stay on local machine. Safe for confidential projects.
